title: 韓詩外傳, Complete
author: Han, Ying, active 150 B.C.
summary: Han Shi Wai Chuan assembles classical interpretations and moral narratives that explicate lines of the Shi Jing. Through dialogues among Confucius, his disciples, ministers, and rulers it offers exemplars of ritual, filial piety, loyalty, integrity, and restraint. Anecdotes—Zengzi’s humility, Zigong’s tests, Yuan Xian’s poverty, Bo Yi and Shu Qi’s steadfastness, Bao Jiao’s austerity, Yan Ying’s cunning, Li Li’s integrity—teach personal cultivation and public duty. Political parables contrast good governance, ritual order, and measured law with cruelty, excess, and collapse. Practical counsel links music, rites, and temperance to cosmic balance, showing how ethical conduct sustains families and states across Zhou eras.

characters.name: 孔子 (Confucius)
characters.lifespan: survives as authoritative teacher in narratives

characters.name: 子貢 (Zigong)
characters.lifespan: active disciple, survives in anecdotes

characters.name: 曾子 (Zengzi)
characters.lifespan: portrayed as exemplar, survives in text

characters.name: 原憲 (Yuan Xian)
characters.lifespan: lives in poverty as moral exemplar

characters.name: 王子比干 (Prince Bigan)
characters.lifespan: killed for loyalty (martyr)

characters.name: 柳下惠 (Liu Xia Hui)
characters.lifespan: dies to demonstrate faith/trust

characters.name: 伯夷 (Bo Yi)
characters.lifespan: dies (starvation/exile) as exemplar

characters.name: 叔齊 (Shu Qi)
characters.lifespan: dies (starvation/exile) as exemplar

characters.name: 鮑焦 (Bao Jiao)
characters.lifespan: abandons worldly gain; fate left bleak in narrative

characters.name: 袁旌目 (?)
characters.lifespan: portrayed historically as exemplar (fate unspecified)

characters.name: 申徒狄 (Shen Tu Di)
characters.lifespan: commits suicide by drowning

characters.name: 崔嘉 (Cui Jia)
characters.lifespan: prevents others' rash acts and survives in anecdote

characters.name: 邵伯 (Shao Bo)

characters.name: 華元 (Huayuan)

characters.name: 晏子 (Yan Ying / Yan Zi)
